description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio with a rectangular wooden body and rounded edges, featuring a weathered, rustic finish. The front panel is painted turquoise and includes a large circular speaker grille on the left, a diagonal metallic accent, and three round control knobs below the grille. A thin, metallic antenna extends vertically from the top left corner, suggesting its function as a radio receiver. The sides and back are plain, showcasing the wood grain texture and aged patina. The radio stands on two short, blocky feet, elevating it slightly above the surface. Its overall design evokes mid-20th-century aesthetics, making it a decorative as well as functional electronic device.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio with a turquoise front panel, large speaker grille, three control knobs, and a vertical antenna. The body is rectangular with rounded edges and a weathered finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the turquoise panel, large speaker grille, three control knobs, and the vertical antenna.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wooden surface and the antenna attachment point.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the unadorned wooden back with visible grain and patina.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the curved wooden side and the radio's blocky feet.
